title: logformatter: better recognition of test names
body:
Ginkgo test names can have more than two levels: there can be
a nested series of Describes() before the final It(). (e.g.,
quadlet_test.go). Handle that.

Before: we just assumed that the third-or-maybe-fourth line
after a "-----" divider was the test name.

Now: examine every line after the "-----" divider, until the
first empty line. Lines with /path/to/source/file are ignored,
lines with text strings are assembled together to make anchors.
This is still imperfect but it's much better than before.

SPECIAL NOTE: in order to allow linking to timing results
in the AfterSuite, I've changed the test name from Leaf to Full.
This will now be a much longer string, and hence much less
readable, but I'm inclined to think it's more correct. Please
review carefully and lmk if I should revert.

Finally, as an unrelated add-on, add links (at top) to original
log, journal, and (if applicable) podman-remote server logs.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
PLEASE DO NOT RESTART ANY FLAKES! Thank you! A flake would be a great way to confirm that the new code is working as desired.  [edit: sigh, except for quay flakes in setup]

Formatting of [Test Bindings](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5256280642682880/html/bindings-podman-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html)  is completely broken, because that log does not have a blank line between test names and test output. Does anyone know how to add a blank separator line? (I will look for myself; am just hoping to save myself time)
I think the difference output is because it runs in sequence and not in parallel. I assume the machine test would face the same problem.
Yes... the blank lines are added by `-p`. I've pushed a kludge workaround.

Good news: a [flake](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5204374922723328/html/sys-remote-fedora-37-root-host-boltdb.log.html). Bad news: it's system tests (unlinkat/ebusy), so it doesn't exercise the new ginkgo-recognizing code. Good news: the new log links (main, journal, remote) are working and very helpful.
No flakes, but machine and bindings tests look better. I call it ready.

Here we go, flake triggered and new logformatter correctly got the full link: https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6405407497781248/html/int-podman-fedora-38-rootless-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-build-podman-system-reset-must-clean-host-shared-cache--1   <--- note the 't--Podman-build' part is the Description(), the rest is the It().
